Fed Fund - Individual Market Analysis

CoT signals and diagnostics for Fed Fund futures.

As of 2026-03-31

Gemini Summary

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement: Given a Spec Net % OI of -2.4%, a 4-week flow of +6.4, and a Spec z-score of 0.13, this setup aligns with Upward-biased price paths and Compressed volatility, where the dominant risk is Trend continuation, not Mean reversion (1).
  • The signal has transitioned from a period of aggressive long reduction into a stabilizing "Normal" regime characterized by a renewed long build (1).
  • Conviction Band: Medium; Interpretation Confidence: High Confidence; Internal Conflict Flag: No. Signal Stability Assessment: Improving; Threshold Proximity: Far; Revision Sensitivity: Moderate.

Methodology Applied:

  • Multi-week flow changes (Flow_4w) are interpreted as a gradual repricing of policy expectations (1).
  • Extreme positioning (Spec_zscore ≥ |2.0|) identifies high vulnerability to policy surprises; current levels indicate low consensus fragility (1).
  • Price stability alongside rising positioning flow suggests a "Long Build" supportive of the current price trend (1).
  • Fed Funds Individual Market Analysis: latest observation 2026-03-31 (1).

Key Dynamics:

  • The primary driver is the reversal in 4-week flow from -18.3 (Feb 03) to +6.4 (Mar 31), indicating a tactical shift back toward long exposure (1).
  • Momentum is stabilizing as Speculator z-scores move from -1.05 toward neutral (0.13), reducing mean-reversion pressure (1).
  • Conditional Invalidation: A sharp weekly flow move exceeding -10.0 would suggest a forced de-leveraging event or a pivot in policy-path consensus (1).
  • Positioning tension has collapsed from 4.31 to 0.31, indicating high internal coherence between participants (1).

Scenario Balance:

  • Base case dominant: Continued gradual long accumulation as market expectations align with Fed guidance.
  • Upside risk: A "Squeeze risk" regime if short covers accelerate alongside bullish macro data.
  • Downside risk: Sudden long liquidation triggered by a hawkish surprise in CPI or employment data.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Tactical (weeks); driven by 4-week flow momentum and weekly positioning shifts (1).
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Medium; current neutrality provides a clean baseline for combining with directional macro themes.

Macro Relevance:

  • Informs the Liquidity and Policy Path dimension by quantifying market consensus on the Fed's rate trajectory (1).
  • The economic mechanism is the repricing of front-end rates, affecting short-term funding costs and USD attractiveness.
  • Cycle position: Not determined.
  • Typical interaction: High sensitivity to 2-Year Treasury yields and USD index momentum (1).

Regime Context:

  • The signal is persistent in a "Normal" state following a transition from "Elevated Risk" in late 2025 (1).
  • Direction of change: Stabilising, as z-scores have moved from historical extremes toward the 52-week mean (1).

Model Limitations:

  • Lagging CoT reporting (data delay) and binary sensitivity to discrete FOMC or CPI calendar events (1).

Data & References:

  • FED FUNDS - CHICAGO BOARD OF TRADE (CoT): 2026-03-31 (1).
  • Most influential: 4-week flow (+6.4) and Spec Net % OI (-2.4%).
  • Relevant external data: 2Y Treasury Yields and VIX Futures (1).
